2606.23436.title: Rethinking Object-Centric Representations for Video Dynamics Modeling
2606.23436.paper_summary: Unsupervised video object tracking aims to decompose dynamic scenes into persistent, object-centric entities without manual annotations. Many recent approaches rely on slot-based representations, where a fixed set of latent variables ("slots") represent individual objects across frames. To preserve object identity, these models enforce temporal consistency on slot embeddings. However, when appearance and pose are entangled, this consistency objective conflicts with object motion and viewpoint changes. As a result, slots tend to lock onto static regions (e.g., background) to satisfy the consistency objective, while foreground objects become fragmented across multiple slots or frequently swap identities. To address these limitations, we propose STAITUS, a unified framework that explicitly disentangles each slot into appearance and geometric pose (position/scale). Leveraging this disentanglement, STAITUS enforces within-frame spatial separation and applies temporal alignment only in appearance space, yielding sharper masks and more persistent identities under motion, occlusion, and object entry/exit. Furthermore, to mitigate over-segmentation, we introduce an adaptive gating mechanism that dynamically adjusts the number of active slots to match scene complexity. Extensive experiments on synthetic and real-world benchmarks demonstrate that STAITUS substantially outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in segmentation quality and tracking stability.
2606.23436.paper_summary_zh: 無監督視頻物體追蹤旨在將動態場景分解為持久的、以物體為中心的實體，而無需人工標註。許多近期的方法依賴於基於插槽的表示，其中一組固定的潛在變量（"插槽"）代表跨幀的個別物體。為了保持物體身份，這些模型對插槽嵌入施加時間一致性。然而，當外觀和姿態交織在一起時，這種一致性目標與物體運動和視角變化發生衝突。因此，插槽往往會鎖定在靜態區域（例如背景）以滿足一致性目標，而前景物體則在多個插槽之間變得碎片化或頻繁交換身份。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了STAITUS，一個統一框架，明確地將每個插槽解耦為外觀和幾何姿態（位置/縮放）。利用這種解耦，STAITUS在幀內強制空間分離，並僅在外觀空間中應用時間對齊，從而在運動、遮擋和物體進出時產生更清晰的掩膜和更持久的身份。此外，為了減少過度分割，我們引入了一種自適應閘控機制，動態調整活動插槽的數量以匹配場景複雜性。在合成和現實世界基準上的廣泛實驗表明，STAITUS在分割質量和追蹤穩定性方面顯著超越了最先進的基準。
2606.23436.author: Amaury Wei et.al.
2606.23436.authors: Amaury Wei, Ismail Nejjar, Olga Fink
